title: Keep migration locks short and weak
description: Schema changes avoid long-held ACCESS EXCLUSIVE locks by splitting steps and setting lock_timeout.
body: Use when running ALTER TABLE or other DDL on a populated table. Do not run a rewriting or validating ALTER that holds ACCESS EXCLUSIVE while a queue of queries piles up behind it. Actions: prefer non-rewriting forms (add a nullable column or one with a constant default on modern PG is fast; adding NOT NULL or a CHECK can be done as NOT VALID then VALIDATE separately, which takes a weaker lock); set a short lock_timeout so a blocked DDL fails fast instead of freezing the table. Validation signals include the migration acquiring its lock quickly and releasing it without a backlog. Risk boundary: a long ACCESS EXCLUSIVE lock on a hot table stalls all traffic; escalate any unavoidable full-table rewrite.
